vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Mount a rear pusher 2814 BLDC motor with 10x7 folding prop clearance
Maintain thrust line +1 degree down
Provide service access and inspection points
Ensure thermal management via cooling inlet
Integrate electrical harness for motor control

generated_parts.label: Center Body Pod
generated_parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

requirements.description: Motor mount must securely hold motor pod.
Bolt circle must provide secure attachment points.
Cooling inlet must allow airflow for motor pod cooling.
Pusher prop location must be accurately defined.
Motor pod must be positioned correctly.
Equipment bay door must provide access.
Wing spar reinforcements must transfer loads.
Service access cut must facilitate maintenance.

verify.value: All required fields are present and non-empty.
verify.rule: Check that all required fields in the schema are filled out.
Ensure the estimated mass matches the design requirements.

verify.value: All required features are mapped to specific geometry operations.
verify.rule: Each feature in the part_tree should have corresponding geometry_ops.

verify.value: All interfaces are reciprocal with neighboring subsystems.
verify.rule: Interfaces should be defined with matching connection types and DOFs.

verify.value: Visible locking methods and preload evidence are provided.
verify.rule: Critical joints must show locking methods and preload/torque intent.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to bolt circle spacing.
risk.mit: Verify bolt circle spacing during pre-assembly checks.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient airflow if cooling inlet is obstructed.
risk.mit: Ensure unobstructed path for cooling air flow.

risk.desc: Possible structural weakness at wing spar reinforcements.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis on wing spar reinforcement areas.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fastener engagement into printed material.
risk.mit: Use inserts or bosses for threaded connections.

risk.desc: Access issues for equipment bay door may hinder maintenance.
risk.mit: Ensure clear access paths and proper labeling for maintenance.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

physics_paths.path: Cooling air flows through cooling_inlet to dissipate heat from motor pod
Wing spar reinforcement transfers loads from wing structure to pod structure
Equipment bay door provides access to equipment bay for maintenance and installation

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: visual and tactile inspection for alignment and fit

verification_focus.expected_result: correct number and spacing of bolts

generated_parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
generated_parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

requirements: Structural integrity under lift, landing, and payload loads
Continuous load path from wingtip to fuselage
Thermal management from sun-exposed surface to wing root
Anti-rotation mechanism during flight maneuvers
Service access for maintenance and repair
Payload integration points
Sensor mounting locations

verify.rule: Each required evidence_feature must appear in part_tree/geometry_ops.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value.
Each part and feature must be traceable from part_tree to geometry_ops.
Name exact neighboring subsystem labels and choose connection_type + DOF + clearance_mm + required_feature.
Critical joints need visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque_Nm or preload intent.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment due to complex assembly order.
risk.mit: Review and confirm assembly order.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal expansion mismatch between CFRP and EPP foam.
risk.mit: Conduct thermal expansion tests.

risk.desc: Insufficient clearance for moving parts may cause jamming.
risk.mit: Increase clearance as necessary.

risk.desc: Inadequate support structures may lead to post-processing issues.
risk.mit: Optimize support orientation.

risk.desc: Potential for visual inspection errors due to complex geometry.
risk.mit: Implement additional inspection methods.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.description: Lift and landing loads transferred from wing skin to fuselage via spar and carry-through.
Heat dissipation from sun-exposed surface to cooler wing root area.
Anti-rotation mechanism ensuring structural integrity during flight maneuvers.

integration_opportunities.description: Access for maintenance and repair of wing spar components.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and axis rings
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented for minimal post-processing
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection and CMM for critical dimensions

verification_focus.description: Ensure the spar does not exceed the allocated weight budget.
Verify the spar can withstand specified loads without failure.
Confirm the assembly order ensures proper fit and function.

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to varying materials (EPP foam core + CFRP spar).
risk.mit: Conduct thermal cycling tests during prototype phase.

risk.desc: Risk of control surface binding if hinge clearance is not precise.
risk.mit: Implement strict quality control on hinge clearance measurements.

risk.desc: Possibility of spar misalignment if mounting datums are not clearly defined.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if load paths are not continuous.

risk.desc: Potential for service access difficulties if holes/pockets are not adequately sized.
risk.mit: Review and adjust hole/pocket sizes based on serviceability requirements.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing planform, airfoil section evidence, winglets, elevon/control surface cutouts with hinges

physics_paths.description: Lift and drag forces from airflow transmitted through the rib to the spar and wing structure.
Heat dissipation from the wing structure to the surrounding air.
Control surface movement via hinge line allowing rotation.

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and tactile

verification_focus.description: Ensure the total mass does not exceed the budgeted 200g per rib.
Verify snap-fit and press-fit interfaces have correct clearances.
Test control surface movement via hinge line.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels
physics_paths: Load path: Servo load transferred to wing structure via servo pocket and control horn
Flow path: Control signal from flight controller to servo via connector mate
Thermal path: Heat dissipation from servo to surrounding air via convection and radiation
integration_opportunities: Optimize servo pocket shape for weight reduction while maintaining structural integrity
Improve access cover design for easier maintenance and replacement of components
Enhance strain relief clamps to protect cables from mechanical stress

manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit

verification_focus: Mass verification: Ensure total mass does not exceed 150g
Fit verification: Confirm all interfaces align correctly during assembly
Functionality verification: Validate servo operation and control horn movement
